methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Dronamics
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Intramotev
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Seneca
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Rainmaker is a Series A drone and autonomous systems company that has raised $25M to date. The broader Drones & Autonomous sector comprises eight private peers—including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air—with no liquid public comparables available for direct benchmarking.

Given the early-stage nature and dispersion within the peer set, only one valuation method proved applicable: stage-progression analysis. Using total capital raised ($25M) as a value proxy and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to subsequent stages yields an implied valuation of $50M. The Peer-Median method was not applicable; while five same-sector peers exist, none satisfied the narrow filters for stage alignment, adjacency, or valuation tier, and a broader cross-sector median would introduce undue noise.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $42M to $57M, with a median point of $50M. This range reflects the primary uncertainty: the absence of sufficiently tight peer comparables limits the ability to cross-check stage-progression assumptions against market precedent in autonomous systems specifically. Key variables affecting any final valuation include unit economics trajectory, technical differentiation, and path to revenue—none of which are reflected in this capitalization-based floor.
